title: birthday warn fires repeatedly if chain tip unavailable at startup
description: Scenario: scanner initializes with scan_tip=0, calls scan_to_tip(), warn\! fires, then latest_height() fails or returns 0 (fresh testnet node, network partition at startup). start=1 > end=0, returns Ok(0). scan_tip stays 0. Next poll: warn\! fires again. Repeats every poll_interval (75s) until the node becomes reachable. In a log aggregator this looks like a continuously alerting condition rather than a one-time setup reminder. Maintenance consequence: users file 'why does my log say scanning from genesis every 75 seconds' reports. Fix: fire the warn\! only once by checking scan_tip==0 before the loop in spawn_scanner, or use a tracing::warn_once\! equivalent, or suppress after the first Ok(0) return by setting a flag on the scanner struct.
close_reason: Fixed: birthday_warned: bool field on CompactBlockScanner; warn! fires at most once per scanner instance
